mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor demand and corporate earnings prospects, which equity markets price in with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative PAYEMS momentum periods show non-negative mean forward returns with p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate is cut by more than 25bp within a single month amid acute financial stress, markets interpret this as emergency liquidity provision, producing a short-lived positive S&P 500 relief rally over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return following qualifying FEDFUNDS cuts is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) across at least 5 historical instances, or is negative in over half of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold indicates a collapsing residential construction sector and impaired household balance sheets, which propagates into broader demand contraction and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following HOUST breaches below threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent multi-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which is negatively priced into equities over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO change and forward 20d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or contraction periods show non-negative mean forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling payrolls (PAYEMS) constitutes a confirmed labor-market downturn signal stronger than either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as recession probability repricing accelerates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >0.2pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are jointly met, mean forward 20d SPX return is not significantly more negative than the single-factor PAYEMS-only signal (delta not significant at p>0.10), indicating no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: During a systemic financial crisis regime, a decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals consumer retrenchment that compounds credit-market dysfunction, producing amplified negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter relative to normal-regime sensitivity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month during crisis regime
regime_hint: systemic financial crisis / credit market dysfunction
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX return sensitivity (regression coefficient) to RSAFS declines during crisis-labeled periods is not statistically distinguishable from non-crisis periods (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month drop in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding a material threshold signals an abrupt consumer demand shock that produces an immediate negative S&P 500 reaction over the following week as analysts revise earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return following qualifying RSAFS declines is not significantly negative (p>0.10) across at least 8 historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of coordinated monetary easing (falling FEDFUNDS) occurring alongside rising unemployment (UNRATE), the market interprets aggressive policy accommodation as insufficient to offset labor deterioration in the near term but sets up a positive rebound over the following quarter as easing takes effect, producing a regime-dependent sign flip in the FEDFUNDS-SPX relationship relative to normal cycles.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls >1pp over 6 months AND UNRATE rises >0.5pp over 6 months
regime_hint: aggressive monetary easing during rising unemployment (crisis response regime)
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns under joint trigger conditions are not significantly more positive than unconditional baseline returns (p>0.10), or sign of relationship matches normal-cycle FEDFUNDS easing effect without regime distinction.
